behavior: Obligate brood parasite; does not build its own nest, instead laying eggs in other birds’ nests. Often forages on the ground and gathers in flocks, especially in open country.
field_marks: Stocky, short-tailed blackbird with a thick, conical, finch-like bill.
Adult male has a rich brown head contrasting with a glossy black body.
Adult female is plain dusty brown, typically paler on the head and underparts, with faint belly streaking.
Dark eye; legs and feet are dark gray to black.
Broad wings and a short, square-to-rounded tail give a compact silhouette.
habitat: Open and semi-open habitats, including grasslands, fields, pastures, meadows, lawns, brushy edges, and woodland margins; generally avoids dense, unbroken forest.

sources.title: Brown-headed Cowbird Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
